subject: Home Economics
context: Understanding who a consumer is and learning about the six universal consumer rights including right to safety, information, choice, be heard, redress, and consumer education

subject: Home Economics
context: Identifying challenges faced by consumers in the marketplace such as poor quality products, overpricing, false advertising, lack of information, and unfair trade practices

subject: Home Economics
context: Learning about different types of redress available to consumers (replacement, refund, repair) and the procedures for seeking redress through appropriate channels and consumer protection agencies

subject: Home Economics
context: Understanding child development as progressive changes in a child from birth to adolescence, learning the stages: infancy (0-2 years), early childhood (2-6 years), middle childhood (6-12 years), adolescence (12-18 years)

subject: Home Economics
context: Exploring factors that affect how children develop including heredity, nutrition, health care, environment, parenting styles, education, socio-economic status, and emotional support

subject: Home Economics
context: Learning about essential conditions for proper child development including adequate nutrition, safe environment, love and affection, education, healthcare, play and recreation, and social interaction

subject: Home Economics
context: Understanding different types of care required by children including physical care (bathing, feeding, clothing), emotional care (love, security), intellectual care (education, stimulation), and social care (interaction, play)

subject: Home Economics
context: Identifying and understanding common childhood diseases and health conditions such as measles, chickenpox, malaria, diarrhea, respiratory infections, and their symptoms, prevention, and basic care

subject: Home Economics
context: Learning about the importance of immunization and the different types required by children according to age including BCG, polio, DPT, hepatitis B, measles, and yellow fever vaccines

subject: Home Economics
context: Understanding what textiles are (materials made from fibers used to make fabrics), learning basic textile terms: fiber (basic unit), yarn (twisted fibers), warp (lengthwise threads), weft (crosswise threads)

subject: Home Economics
topic: Reasons for Studying Textiles and Importance
context: Exploring why we study textiles and their importance in daily life including clothing, home furnishings, protection, cultural expression, economic value, and career opportunities

subject: Home Economics
context: Differentiating between natural fibers (cotton, wool, silk, linen from plants and animals) and synthetic fibers (nylon, polyester, acrylic made from chemicals), understanding their properties and characteristics

subject: Home Economics
topic: Properties, Care and Uses of Different Fibers
context: Learning specific properties of natural fibers (absorbent, breathable, biodegradable) and synthetic fibers (durable, wrinkle-resistant, quick-drying), their appropriate uses and proper care methods

subject: Home Economics
context: Understanding how textiles are produced through weaving process, practical hands-on activity of producing woven table mats using strips of paper or cardboard to learn basic weaving techniques

subject: Home Economics
context: Learning proper fabric laundering techniques including sorting clothes, choosing appropriate detergents, washing methods, rinsing, drying, starching, and ironing different types of fabrics

subject: Home Economics
context: Identifying different types of sewing machines (hand-operated, electric, electronic) and learning the parts of a sewing machine including needle, presser foot, bobbin, feed dog, tension disc, and their functions

subject: Home Economics
topic: Choosing and Caring for Sewing Machines
context: Understanding factors to consider when choosing a sewing machine (purpose, budget, features, brand) and learning proper care and maintenance including cleaning, oiling, changing needles, and troubleshooting

subject: Home Economics
context: Learning terms used in garment construction such as facing (fabric finish for edges), hems (folded and sewn edge), openings (zippers, buttons), fastenings, darts (tapered folds), tucks, and seams

subject: Home Economics
topic: Using Sewing Machine for Garment Construction
context: Practical lessons in operating a sewing machine to make straight and curved stitches, practicing on fabric pieces, and understanding machine settings for different fabrics and stitches

subject: Home Economics
context: Step-by-step practical project of constructing a simple baby dress using a sewing machine, applying learned skills in pattern reading, cutting, stitching, and finishing garments

subject: Home Economics
topic: Food Hygiene and Safety - Meaning and Importance
context: Understanding food hygiene as practices that prevent food contamination, learning reasons for healthy food handling including preventing foodborne illnesses, maintaining nutritional value, and ensuring consumer safety

subject: Home Economics
context: Learning safe ways to handle food including washing hands before cooking, keeping utensils clean, storing food properly, covering food, separating raw and cooked foods, and cooking food thoroughly

subject: Home Economics
context: Understanding sources of food contamination (bacteria, viruses, chemicals, physical objects) and food poisoning, learning the effects of consuming contaminated food on health and prevention methods

subject: Home Economics
context: Identifying spoilage signs using senses: sight (mold, discoloration), smell (off odors, sour smell), taste (unusual flavors), texture (sliminess, softness), and understanding causative micro-organisms like bacteria, molds, and yeasts

subject: Home Economics
context: Understanding the differences between snacks (small portions eaten between meals) and main meals (substantial portions eaten at regular times), their nutritional roles and appropriate timing in daily diet

subject: Home Economics
context: Learning to prepare common snacks and drinks such as meat pies, puff-puff, chin-chin, samosa, zobo drink, and fruit juices, understanding recipes, ingredients, and proper food preparation techniques

subject: Home Economics
context: Learning how to properly package prepared food items for storage, sale, or gifting using appropriate materials like picnic boxes, cellophane bags, plastic containers, ensuring food safety and attractive presentation

subject: Home Economics
context: Understanding basic marketing principles including identifying target customers, pricing strategies, product quality, packaging appeal, hygiene standards, advertising, and customer service for selling food products

subject: Home Economics
topic: Duties of Food Sellers and Providers
context: Learning the responsibilities of food sellers and providers for public consumption including maintaining hygiene, providing quality food, honest pricing, proper labeling, following food safety regulations, and customer care

subject: Home Economics
topic: Responsible Food Management and Safety Regulations
context: Understanding responsibilities of food safety managers to consumers, differentiating between hygienic and unhygienic food handling methods, learning about NAFDAC regulations and the effects of poor food management on public health
